nodes.name: Edolisoma
nodes.summary: Edolisoma（Edolisoma）是图谱中的分类级分类节点，当前关联30种鸟类，如山鹃鵙、菲律宾黑鹃鵙、白翅鹃鵙等30种鸟类。

nodes.name: South Moluccan cicadabird
nodes.summary: The South Moluccan cicadabird (Edolisoma amboinense) is a passerine bird in the family Campephagidae found on the islands of Buru, Ambon, and Seram in the Maluku Islands of Indonesia. The species was formerly considered conspecific with the common cicadabird, now renamed the Sahul cicadabird. Taxonomy The South Moluccan cicadabird was formally described in 1865 by German ornithologist Gustav Hartlaub based on a specimen collected on Ambon Island (Ambon) in the Maluku Islands. He coined the binomial name Ceblepyris amboinense. The South Moluccan cicadabird was formerly treated as conspecific with the common cicadabird (now renamed the Sahul cicadabird) (Edolisoma tenuirostre) but has since been elevated to species status. The species is monotypic: no subspecies are recognised. References
